raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "prescribing acceptance of wakefulness via a conditional behavioral prediction to reduce sleep-related striving",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "absence of effortful striving produces a calm behavioral state",
    "lying quietly constitutes a desirable or at least non-problematic outcome",
    "the speaker can reliably inhibit effort once the conditional is adopted",
    "quiet wakefulness is within voluntary control"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as a simple conditional; the consequent follows logically from the antecedent under an operant or paradoxical-intention frame, though it leaves open whether sleep itself will occur",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the recipient experiences sleep-related performance anxiety or hyperarousal; adopting the statement reliably lowers effort and associated distress; the therapeutic goal is acceptance of wakefulness rather than direct sleep induction",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the recipient interprets the statement as prohibiting any sleep-conducive behavior; the statement is taken as a literal guarantee that sleep will never occur; cultural or individual expectations equate lying quietly with failure or wasted time"
}
